title: Pure Render Logic and State Batching

learningObjectives.text: Explain pure components using a new example and justify the result.
Explain render-time side effects to avoid using a new example and justify the result.
Apply batched state updates using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: This topic turns pure render logic and state batching from isolated terminology into a practical tool for reading, writing, and reviewing code. It belongs to the **How React Renders** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Pure components** → **Render-time side effects to avoid** → **Batched state updates**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: Pure components
sections.body: Pure render logic returns the same description for the same inputs and does not mutate external systems.

sections.title: Render-time side effects to avoid
sections.body: Network requests, subscriptions, timers, DOM mutations, and external writes do not belong in render because render may be repeated or abandoned.

sections.title: Batched state updates
sections.body: React groups multiple updates before rendering when possible. Updater functions reliably compose transitions based on earlier queued values.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.explanation: Updater functions compose correctly when React batches the event updates.

commonMistakes: Mutating props or state instead of producing the next value.
Adding state or an Effect for a value that can be derived during render.
Using global state before identifying the real owner and consumers.
Optimizing renders without a measured user-facing problem.
summary: Pure Render Logic and State Batching connects Pure components, Render-time side effects to avoid, Batched state updates.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

assessmentProfile.label: Code-reading, debugging, and implementation challenge
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic represents a day-to-day coding skill. The topic quiz must primarily test code reading, debugging, output prediction, or implementation choice.
assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.
